project_info.creator: Balthasar Hoettges
project_info.purpose: AI-powered ARR forecasting for venture capital, combining LightGBM with a rule-based health assessment for edge cases.

model_details.prediction_target: ARR YoY growth for the next 4 quarters

performance.confidence_intervals: ±10% for standard trajectories, ±25% for volatile cases

model_details.algorithm_type: Hybrid Forecasting: LightGBM for growth-regime companies, Rule-Based Health Assessment for edge cases

algorithm_explanation.overview: A hybrid forecasting system that routes each prediction through a 6-factor trend analysis. Standard growth patterns go to a LightGBM ML model (R² ≈ 0.85); edge cases (decline, volatility, stagnation, reversals) are routed to a transparent Rule-Based Health Assessment that scores company fundamentals against industry benchmarks.

stage_1_tier_based_input.description: Tier 1 (Required): Q1-Q4 ARR, Headcount, Sector. Tier 2 (Optional): Gross Margin, Sales & Marketing, Cash Burn, Churn Rate, Customers, Expansion Rate.
stage_1_tier_based_input.benefit: Minimal user input required — the system always produces a forecast with just Tier 1 data, while rewarding higher-quality inputs with increased fidelity.

stage_2_intelligent_feature_completion.description: The LightGBM model expects ~150 features, but users provide at most a dozen. The system bridges this gap by finding the 50 most similar companies in the training data (via log-space ARR similarity, growth similarity, and headcount similarity) and imputing missing metrics using similarity-weighted medians.
process: 1. Company Profiling: Score every training company on ARR scale, growth rate, and headcount similarity
2. Peer Selection: Retain the top 50 companies by composite similarity score
3. Weighted Median Imputation: For each missing feature, compute the similarity-weighted median from the peer set
4. Feature Engineering: Construct ~150 engineered features including temporal lags, rolling statistics, SaaS efficiency ratios, and growth trajectory features
features_created: Temporal Lags: 1-, 2-, and 4-quarter lags for ARR, burn, margins, headcount, and growth
Rolling Statistics: 4-quarter rolling means and standard deviations (shifted to prevent leakage)
SaaS Efficiency Ratios: Magic Number, Burn Multiple, ARR per Headcount
Growth Trajectory: log_cARR, growth momentum, QoQ ARR ratio and its lag, 4-quarter rolling growth mean
Domain Features: Headcount QoQ growth, sector encoding

stage_3_modeling.why_lightgbm: Excellent for heterogeneous tabular data, robust under imputation noise, captures non-linear SaaS interactions implicitly, fast CPU inference
stage_3_modeling.validation: Company-based holdout split (80/20) — no company appears in both train and test. 5-fold GroupKFold cross-validation for robust R² estimation.

confidence_intervals.method: ±10% for standard trajectories, ±25% for volatile cases
confidence_intervals.rationale: Pragmatic decision-support ranges calibrated to the uncertainty profile of each trajectory type
confidence_intervals.application: Applied to both individual quarter predictions and cumulative growth forecasts

features: Tier 2 (Optional): Gross Margin, Sales & Marketing, Cash Burn, Churn Rate, Customers, Expansion Rate
Engineered: ~150 features including lags, rolling windows, SaaS ratios, growth trajectory features
data_description.notes: Target values winsorized at ±500% before training. Feature winsorization (1st/99th percentiles) and median imputation computed on training data only.

limitations: Predictions are based on historical data and may not reflect future market conditions or structural regime shifts.
The model is trained predominantly on growth-regime VC-backed SaaS companies; performance may vary for other profiles.
Peer-based imputation may reinforce cohort-level correlations rather than recover each company's true metrics.
disclaimer: This tool is for informational and academic purposes only and does not constitute financial advice.

faqs.question: How accurate are the predictions?
faqs.answer: The LightGBM model achieves R² ≈ 0.85 on the held-out test set and R² ≈ 0.77 via 5-fold cross-validation. Predictions include ±10% decision-support bounds for standard trajectories and ±25% for volatile cases.

faqs.question: What happens with declining or volatile companies?
faqs.answer: A 6-factor trend analysis classifies the company's trajectory. Declining, volatile, flat, or reversing companies are routed to a Rule-Based Health Assessment instead of the ML model. This scores the company on 5 pillars (ARR Growth, NRR, CAC Payback, Rule of 40, Runway) and produces benchmark-driven projections.

faqs.question: What is intelligent feature completion?
faqs.answer: When you provide minimal data, the system finds the 50 most similar companies from the training dataset using log-space ARR similarity, growth similarity, and headcount similarity. Missing features are imputed using similarity-weighted medians from that peer set, preserving realistic conditional correlations.
